entries.title: The 2025 AI Index Report
entries.abstract: The 2025 AI Index Report provides a rigorous, objective analysis of AI development and deployment across technical, economic, and social dimensions. Key findings document continued improvements in AI model performance on demanding benchmarks, rapid integration of AI into commercial and healthcare applications, record private investment particularly in generative AI, and growing regulatory attention from governments worldwide. The report also identifies significant regional disparities in public sentiment toward AI and uneven progress in responsible AI practices despite rising incident rates.

entries.title: The 2024 AI Index Report
entries.subtitle: A comprehensive annual analysis of trends in AI research, development, economy, governance, and societal impact across multiple dimensions.
entries.abstract: The 2024 AI Index Report, now in its seventh edition, provides a comprehensive examination of artificial intelligence trends across nine major domains: research and development, technical performance, responsible AI, economics, science and medicine, education, policy and governance, diversity, and public opinion. The report synthesizes data from numerous authoritative sources to track AI's technical advancements, commercial development, regulatory landscape, and societal perceptions. Key findings indicate that while AI has surpassed human performance on specific benchmarks, industry dominance in frontier model development continues to accelerate, with training costs reaching unprecedented levels and the United States maintaining leadership in notable AI model production.

entries.title: The 2023 AI Index Report
entries.subtitle: An independent comprehensive analysis of artificial intelligence trends across research, development, economics, policy, and society.
entries.publisher: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI)
entries.abstract: The 2023 AI Index Report, produced by Stanford's Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence, provides a comprehensive empirical analysis of global artificial intelligence trends across eight major dimensions: research and development, technical performance, AI ethics, economics, education, policy and governance, diversity, and public opinion. The report documents significant shifts in AI development, including industry dominance over academia in model production, performance saturation on traditional benchmarks, growing environmental concerns, and rapidly increasing incidents of AI misuse. Drawing on data from over 50 organizations and expanding coverage from 25 to 127 countries, the report offers evidence-based insights for policymakers and stakeholders to advance AI responsibly.

entries.title: The 2022 AI Index Report
entries.subtitle: An annual comprehensive assessment of artificial intelligence progress, adoption, and governance across research, technology, economy, policy, and ethics dimensions.
entries.publisher: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI)
entries.abstract: The 2022 AI Index Report, produced by the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence, provides a multidisciplinary analysis of global artificial intelligence development and deployment. The report synthesizes data from academic, private, and nonprofit organizations across five major dimensions: research and development, technical performance, AI ethics, economy and education, and policy and governance. Key findings indicate accelerating private investment concentration, emerging disparities in model capability versus bias trade-offs, expanding AI ethics research, declining AI implementation costs, and increasing legislative activity around artificial intelligence across 25 countries.

entries.title: The 2021 AI Index Report
entries.abstract: The 2021 AI Index Report presents an expanded dataset measuring trends in artificial intelligence development across technical performance, economic indicators, and human capital. The report analyzes COVID-19's impact on AI research and development, finding that private investment and conference participation actually increased during the pandemic. Key findings address investment patterns in drug discovery, workforce demographics and diversity challenges, international talent flows, advances in generative AI systems, surveillance technology proliferation, and gaps in AI ethics benchmarking and consensus.

entries.title: The 2019 AI Index Report
entries.subtitle: A comprehensive data-driven analysis tracking artificial intelligence research, development, economics, and societal impacts across global metrics.
entries.abstract: The 2019 AI Index Report provides an unbiased, rigorous compilation of data on artificial intelligence activities, trends, and performance across nine key dimensions: research and development, conferences, technical performance, economy, education, autonomous systems, public perception, societal considerations, and national strategies. The report tracks three times as many datasets as the 2018 edition and incorporates input from over 150 industry and academic experts to minimize measurement bias. It includes the Global AI Vibrancy Tool, which benchmarks 28 countries across 34 indicators to facilitate informed policy discussions and public understanding of the AI field.

entries.title: The 2018 AI Index Report
entries.subtitle: A comprehensive data-driven report aggregating metrics on artificial intelligence progress across academic and industry sectors.
entries.abstract: The 2018 AI Index Report provides a systematic collection of diverse metrics to ground conversations about artificial intelligence progress in empirical data. The report aggregates quantitative measures across four major categories: volume of activity (conference attendance, venture capital investment), technical performance (image recognition, theorem proving), derivative measures (trends and the AI Vibrancy Index), and progress toward human performance. The report aims to make underlying data accessible to practitioners, policymakers, and the general public to facilitate informed discourse on AI development and impact.

entries.title: The 2017 AI Index Report
entries.abstract: The 2017 AI Index Report aggregates diverse datasets to measure progress and activity in artificial intelligence across multiple dimensions, including conference attendance, venture capital investment, technical performance benchmarks, and comparative human performance metrics. The report introduces the AI Vibrancy Index as an exploratory measure combining academic and industry trends to quantify the field's dynamism. It includes subjective commentary from AI experts to contextualize quantitative findings and acknowledges the challenges of comprehensively tracking an rapidly evolving field. The report serves as a foundational effort toward establishing rigorous methodologies for measuring future AI progress.

entries.title: AI Index Report 2026
entries.abstract: This report from Stanford HAI presents findings on AI's accelerating capabilities, competitive landscape, and societal adoption. It documents that AI capability continues to advance with industry-produced frontier models achieving human-level performance on complex tasks, while highlighting critical gaps in responsible AI development and uneven global adoption patterns. The analysis covers technical performance, infrastructure concentration, economic investment, workforce dynamics, and policy responses across global markets.

entries.title: Regulating Artificial Intelligence: U.S. and International Approaches and Considerations for Congress
entries.subtitle: An overview of AI regulation, federal policy approaches, and stakeholder perspectives in the United States
entries.abstract: This document examines the current state of artificial intelligence regulation and policy in the United States, noting the absence of comprehensive federal AI legislation while highlighting targeted provisions passed by recent Congresses. It discusses how different administrations have prioritized AI engagement differently, with the Biden Administration emphasizing AI safety and the Trump Administration focusing on security concerns. The document frames the central policy debate around balancing AI innovation opportunities with risk mitigation regarding bias and accuracy.

entries.title: GPTZero finds 100 new hallucinations in NeurIPS 2025 accepted papers
entries.subtitle: A comprehensive analysis revealing widespread hallucinated citations in papers accepted at a top-tier machine learning conference despite rigorous peer review processes.
entries.abstract: This report documents 100 confirmed hallucinated citations discovered across 51-53 papers accepted and published at NeurIPS 2025, identifying a critical vulnerability in academic peer review systems overwhelmed by AI-generated submissions. The analysis examines the systemic failure of the review pipeline to detect fabricated sources, including fake authors, nonexistent articles, and falsified DOIs and URLs. The authors contextualize these findings within a broader crisis of scale, noting a 220% increase in NeurIPS submissions between 2020 and 2025, and argue that traditional peer review mechanisms were not designed to defend against AI-enabled academic fraud at this scale.

entries.title: State Platform Regulation: A Roadmap to Coming Debates
entries.subtitle: An analysis of state-led technology policy development across privacy, content moderation, antitrust, child safety, and taxation.
entries.publisher: NYU Center for Social Media, AI, and Politics
entries.abstract: This report examines the emerging role of U.S. states as primary drivers of technology policy development, particularly following Congressional gridlock on sector-wide governance reforms. Drawing on analysis of 28 platform regulation bills enacted over two years, the document maps state legislative activity across five key domains: privacy, content regulation, antitrust enforcement, child safety protections, and digital taxation. The report projects increased state-level technology policymaking in subsequent legislative sessions, driven by political alignment in trifecta governments and anticipated pressure surrounding reproductive rights and online speech regulation.

entries.title: State Technology Policy in 2023: An Overview of Legislative Developments
entries.subtitle: Analysis of 65 technology policy laws passed by U.S. states during 2023 legislative sessions and emerging state-level policy trends.
entries.publisher: NYU Center for Social Media, AI, and Politics
entries.abstract: This report examines technology policy developments across U.S. states during 2023, a year in which state legislatures took the lead in tech policy reform while federal efforts stalled on issues like privacy and online child safety. The authors document 65 technology policy laws passed by states and analyze changes in the political architecture of state-level technology governance. The report provides an overview of key legislation across six policy topics and relevant state litigation activity, representing the second installment in a series on state technology policy trends.

entries.title: State Technology Policy Developments: Third Annual Report
entries.subtitle: An overview of state-level technology policy changes, legislative developments, and litigation during 2024.
entries.publisher: NYU Center for Social Media, AI, and Politics
entries.abstract: This report provides a comprehensive analysis of developments in state technology policy during 2024, examining the evolving political architecture at the state legislative level. The authors review significant legislative and litigation developments in state tech policy, building on previous reports in this ongoing series. The document synthesizes key policy changes and legal developments affecting technology regulation across United States jurisdictions.

entries.title: The Center on Technology Policy: State Technology Policy Report 2025
entries.publisher: NYU Center for Social Media, AI, and Politics
entries.abstract: This report provides an overview of developments in state technology policy over the year of 2025. It analyzes the changing political architecture at the state level during the 2025 legislative sessions, reviews key developments in state technology legislation and litigation during that same year, and provides predictions on what the state policy landscape could look like in 2026.

entries.title: Ethical Principles for Review of Normothermic Regional Perfusion (NRP) in Medical Transplantation
entries.subtitle: An analysis of ethical considerations and compliance concerns for a new organ preservation technology that aims to increase transplant availability.
entries.abstract: This paper examines the ethical framework applicable to normothermic regional perfusion (NRP), an emerging medical technology that circulates blood through organs after circulatory death to improve organ viability for transplantation. While NRP demonstrates utility benefits through reduced organ non-use, the procedure raises significant ethical concerns regarding compliance with the Dead Donor Rule and adherence to principles of respect for persons and nonmaleficence. The authors recommend comprehensive informed consent procedures that address the unique aspects of NRP, and caution against uncontrolled implementation that could compromise ethical safeguards in the transition from therapeutic treatment to organ recovery.
